reasoning: Response returns 386 Wikipedia/Wikidata hanabi festivals — correct tool, broad coverage. Most items lack prefecture, coordinates, and English descriptions; the list is effectively unsorted raw data. Agent cannot help user pick a location without additional filtering. RULE C: authoritative data but severely incomplete metadata drops practical_usefulness and specificity to 2. No language mismatch (query en, lang en).
missing_data: English names, dates/months, coordinates, and prefectures for most entries; no ranking by prominence or season
improvement_hint: Add month/season filtering so user can narrow to summer dates; surface top-ranked hanabi by prominence_score; auto-translate name_ja to name when name_en is absent

reasoning: Identical response structure to L3-01 but lang=ja. Query is Japanese so language matches. Same 386 hanabi items with sparse metadata. Authoritative source but bulk of items missing coordinates, prefecture, and dates. RULE C applies: real data, incomplete scope.
missing_data: Dates/months, coordinates for most entries, regional clustering
improvement_hint: Provide month-filter parameter; promote items with full metadata (coordinates + prefecture) to top of results

reasoning: Query asks broadly about snow festivals in Japan; tool filtered to Hokkaido only (notable caveat — misses Niigata, Akita Kamakura, Aomori Nebuta-snow variants). canonical_festivals block surfaces Sapporo Snow Festival, Asahikawa Winter Festival, Otaru Snow Light Path — all correct, authoritative, with English descriptions and QIDs. No local Schema.org events but advisory explains why. RULE A: response is useful within Hokkaido scope.
missing_data: Snow festivals outside Hokkaido (Niigata Yukiguni, Akita Kamakura festival, Aomori-area winter festivals)
improvement_hint: Add region_fanout or multi-prefecture suggestion when query is not explicitly geo-restricted; canonical_festivals for Niigata/Akita would fill coverage gap

reasoning: Chinese query about rural countryside living; tool called is get_japan_heritage (all 104 stories) with no filter — wrong tool per RULE A. However the response includes a well-formed browse_advisory that explicitly names the canonical alternatives (get_hotels kind=minshuku, search_area 農泊, 合掌造り) matching the intent. RULE A: wrong tool but the advisory delivers actionable redirects worth partial credit; affected dims (intent, constraint, specificity) drop by 1, not compounded. RULE D: query is zh, response advisory text is en/ja — constraint_handling -1.
missing_data: Actual minshuku/farmstay listings; rural accommodation data
improvement_hint: Intent classifier should detect 乡村生活 and route directly to get_hotels kind=minshuku or search_area 農泊 instead of get_japan_heritage

reasoning: Korean query about autumn foliage (紅葉). search_area q=紅葉 returns 140 results but top items are named '紅葉谷川庭園砂防施設' (an engineering facility) and generic parks named after the kanji — not classic koyo spots like Nikko, Kyoto Eikan-do, or Arashiyama. RULE D: query in ko, response in en/ja — constraint_handling -1. Ranking failure buries the actual famous autumn-leaf destinations under name-match noise. RULE B: single root cause = ranking failure.
missing_data: Famous autumn-leaf destinations (Nikko, Kyoto Arashiyama, Shinjuku Gyoen); Korean-language descriptions
improvement_hint: Add seasonal tag autumn_foliage or koyo_meisho kind filter; deprioritise items whose name contains 紅葉 merely as a toponym; surface kind=garden/park with autumn leaf heritage designations

reasoning: Query explicitly asks for non-touristy, quiet meditation — i.e., wants secluded temples, not tourist sites. Results include a ninja experience at 赤目四十八滝 and a DMO event page for 臨済寺 special-visit tour (¥3,000 tourist experience) — these contradict the 'not touristy' constraint. Top Wikidata hits (修行寺, 修行院) are legitimate but lack descriptions and coordinates. RULE B: constraint failure is the single worst dim; specificity also hurt.
missing_data: Quiet mountain temples known for zazen (e.g. Eiheiji, Sojiji, Koyasan temples); accommodation-style shukubo listings
improvement_hint: Add crowd_band=low filter and exclude dmo_website event-page results when query implies non-touristy; consider kind=zen_temple or shukubo entity class

reasoning: Aurora query in Spanish. Server correctly fires not_available block explaining auroras are not visible from Japan, then offers dark_sky/observatory alternatives. Safety and factual accuracy excellent. RULE D: query in es, all response content in en/ja — constraint_handling -1 for language mismatch; this is the single dim drop per RULE B. Dark-sky alternatives (Sendai Observatory, national parks) are real but not all relevant to an aurora substitute.
missing_data: Spanish-language explanation of why auroras aren't visible; Spanish descriptions of alternative dark-sky spots
wrong_data: Kushiro-shitsugen and Yanbaru national parks listed as alternatives but they lack dark-sky designation in their kinds
improvement_hint: Serve not_available.reason_es in addition to en/ja; filter alternative results to those with dark_sky or observatory kinds rather than generic national parks

reasoning: Query asks specifically for 100-year-old historic houses (古民家) to sleep in. Tool called is get_hotels(prefecture=Gifu) with no kind filter — returns generic hotels: onsen ryokan (hito_yu_kai), an Airbnb apartment, a hostel. Zero results match 古民家 (machiya/kominka). RULE A: wrong tool choice (should be get_hotels kind=kominka or search_area 古民家 宿泊). Data is authoritative Gifu lodging but entirely misses the intent. Score 2 for intent and practical_usefulness; constraint_handling 2 (key constraint 'historic house' completely ignored).
missing_data: Kominka (古民家) lodging options; machiya stays; historic farmhouse guesthouses (Shirakawa-go gassho-zukuri stays)
improvement_hint: Add kind=kominka or kind=machiya filter to get_hotels; intent classifier should detect '100-year-old house' / 古民家 and append kind filter automatically

reasoning: Thai query asking for non-touristy hidden cherry blossom spots. Results include 兼六園 (Kenroku-en) and 姫路城 (Himeji Castle) — the most famous and most crowded sakura spots in Japan, directly contradicting the 穴場 (off-the-beaten-path) constraint. RULE D: query in th, response in en/ja — constraint_handling -1. Ranking prioritises prominence over novelty, inverting the user's request. RULE B: single root cause is constraint failure on the 'not touristy' dimension.
missing_data: Lesser-known sakura spots with low crowd_band; regional hidden gems
improvement_hint: Detect 穴場/hidden/non-touristy intent and apply crowd_band=low filter; demote must_see tier items when user explicitly asks for non-famous spots

reasoning: Photography/landscape query. search_area q=絶景 returns 53 results dominated by DMO feature-article pages ('世界の絶景・詩歩がお勧める観光ルート', '絶景に出会う') rather than named specific landscape spots. reasoning: Arabic query for outdoor hot springs (not hotels). Results include 宝泉坊温泉 (outdoor rotenburo with description), 祓川温泉 (family baths in nature), and 小薮温泉 (registered cultural property with nature views) — good matches to the outdoor/natural constraint. However, query is in Arabic (ar) and all descriptions returned are in Japanese — RULE D: constraint_handling -1. Response otherwise well-targeted to outdoor onsen.

reasoning: Wild dolphin swim query. canonical_wildlife_sites block leads with Mikurajima (wild swim, permit-based, 4-11 months) and Ogasawara (wild swim, UNESCO) — exactly what the user needs. Amakusa clearly labelled watch-only (no swim allowed). Accurate permit/season/access info. Lexical results (aquariums) correctly de-ranked by the canonical block note. RULE E does not apply (no featured_cluster) but canonical_wildlife_sites performs the same function. Travel feasibility minor caveat: overnight ferry to both sites not flagged upfront.
missing_data: Ferry duration/booking info for Mikurajima and Ogasawara
improvement_hint: Add ferry access time and booking URL to canonical_wildlife_sites entries for remote island destinations

reasoning: Query about traditional architecture untouched by modernization — the ideal tool would be search_area q=重伝建 or get_spots with preservation_district kind. Tool called is get_japan_heritage with no filter, returning all 104 日本遺産 stories. 日本遺産 stories are thematic narrative heritage, not the same as 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 (重伝建) preservation districts. browse_advisory redirects are generic and don't specifically name 重伝建. RULE A: partially relevant data returned but wrong scope.
missing_data: 重伝建 (Important Preservation Districts for Traditional Buildings) list; named well-preserved townscapes (Takehara, Hagi, Ouchi-juku)
improvement_hint: Add a browse_advisory entry for 'traditional architecture / preservation district' intent pointing to search_area q=重伝建 or get_spots kind=preservation_district

reasoning: Vietnamese query about Ainu culture in Hokkaido. search_area q=アイヌ returns National Ainu Museum (Upopoy), Nibutani Ainu Culture Museum, Kawamura Kaneto Ainu Museum — all Hokkaido-based, factually correct, with coordinates. RULE D: query in vi, response in en/ja — constraint_handling -1. Coverage is good for museum options but lacks craft-experience or cultural performance listings.

reasoning: Cliff-carved Buddhist statues (磨崖仏) query. search_area returns Usuki Stone Buddhas (National Treasure, prominence 0.9), Oya-dera (Tochigi, prominence 0.9), Komazaka Magaibutsu (Shiga), Kiyomizu Magaibutsu (Kagoshima) — highly relevant, with heritage designations, coordinates. Excellent specificity. Minor caveat: descriptions are null for several top entries, limiting the agent's ability to provide rich context without a follow-up get_description call.

improvement_hint: Auto-trigger get_description for top-ranked entries lacking description_en; add typical_visit_minutes for magaibutsu entries

reasoning: Query specifies single-day hike from Tokyo. Tool returns 88 volcano results including Mt. Iwate (Iwate Prefecture, ~4h from Tokyo), Lake Towada (Aomori), Mt. Unzen (Nagasaki) — none viable as day trips from Tokyo. Mt. Fuji appears but is not a quick day hike for most visitors. RULE B: single worst dim is constraint failure on 'from Tokyo day-trip' — travel_feasibility and constraint_handling both 2, but picking constraint_handling as primary per RULE B. No Tokyo-proximity filtering applied.

reasoning: Indonesian query about eating freshly made tofu at a temple (寺 + 豆腐). Tool searches q=豆腐 returning MAFF GI frozen tofu products, regional tofu producers (壱州豆腐, 金剛山まつまさ), and a recipe page — relevant to tofu but zero results about temple tofu experiences (shojin ryori, Nanzen-ji Yudofu, Eikan-do). reasoning: Query about 'fishing villages frozen in time' — wants preserved historic fishing ports, not active commercial harbours. search_area q=漁港 returns: a 由比港 sakura-ebi food guide, 糸満漁港 (active industrial port), 寺泊 fish market, 白浜漁港 (closed to public). These are modern working ports or food tourism pages, not preserved historic villages (e.g. Ine no Funaya, Tomonoura, Ogi). Ranking surfaces wrong entity type. missing_data: Preserved historic fishing villages: 伊根の舟屋 (Kyoto), 鞆の浦 (Hiroshima), 小木 (Sado); 重伝建 designation for fishing-port districts
improvement_hint: Add kind=historic_fishing_village or query expansion from 漁港 to 伊根 舟屋 鞆の浦 preservation_district; filter for 重伝建 entries in fishing-port category

reasoning: Query asks specifically about washi (和紙) paper making workshops. reasoning: Time-drama landscape (時代劇ロケ地) query in Japanese. search_area q=宿場 returns Tsumago-juku, Narai-juku, Seki-juku — the canonical post-town preservation districts with shukuba + preservation_district kind tags and 重伝建 designations. Minor caveat: expression_quality reduced slightly as some scrape content is raw Japanese with no English translation.
improvement_hint: Add English descriptions to post-town attraction records; include Edo-period film-set context in featured_cluster if available

reasoning: Query asks for uncrowded beaches. Results are dominated by Okinawa beaches (波の上ビーチ, 砂山ビーチ, トロピカルビーチ) — all well-known tourist beaches. crowd_band field shows 'medium' or 'low' but the featured Naminoue Beach in Naha and Sunayama in Miyakojima are tourist-frequented. reasoning: Izakaya/yokocho query. search_area q=横丁 returns 横丁公園 (a park, not a bar alley), a Saga tourism page in Chinese, and theme park attractions (はい!からっと横丁, なにわ食いしんぼ横丁). reasoning: Firefly viewing query. Results include 美郷のホタルおよびその発生地 (Tokushima, Natural Monument), 豊田ホタルの里ミュージアム (Yamaguchi), and Nagasaki/Wakayama scrape content with viewing etiquette notes. Summer timing matches the query.

reasoning: Russian query about wintering cranes. Top result is 出水ツル渡来地 (Izumi, Kagoshima) — the #1 crane wintering ground in Japan with 10,000+ cranes, correctly tagged crane_wintering with Ramsar designation. However subsequent results flood with municipality names containing '鶴' (Tsuru/Crane kanji in place names: 鶴田町, 鶴居村, 鶴ヶ島市 etc.) and unrelated national parks (Kushiro, Yanbaru). RULE D: query in ru, response in en/ja — constraint_handling -1. Ranking buries the actual answer (Tsurui Village / 鶴居村 in Hokkaido also relevant) under municipality noise.
missing_data: 鶴居村 (Hokkaido) as second key crane site; Russian-language descriptions; winter timing (Oct-March) for Izumi
improvement_hint: Suppress municipality-name matches when query is about a wildlife species; elevate crane_wintering kind matches over place-name lexical matches; add 鶴居村 to canonical wildlife sites
